user_query:
I saved two historical makeup tutorials and a rough outreach message. Please help me turn them into a concise guide and polished messages.

Deliverables and constraints:
1) Extract structured technique data from input/articles/1930s_hollywood.html and input/articles/victorian_stage.md into output/techniques.json. The JSON must be an array of objects, each with these fields: source_file (string), era (string), technique_name (string), materials (array of strings), steps (array of strings), cautions (array of strings; empty if none). Base the extraction only on the provided files—do not invent facts.
2) Using output/techniques.json as the sole source, write output/guide.md. Include: a short intro (≤120 words) tailored to a history-loving cosplayer; then, for each technique, a section titled "Era — Technique Name" with subsections Quick Steps, Materials, and Cautions derived from the extracted fields (you may lightly rewrite for clarity but stay faithful to the content). End with a Sources section listing the source_file names.
3) Rewrite input/messages/outreach_draft.txt into a clear, polite, and concise email (≤180 words) saved to output/email_to_historian.txt. Improve tone and brevity while preserving intent. Include one sentence that references the techniques by era and name exactly as they appear in output/techniques.json.
4) Draft a short forum DM (≤120 words) saved to output/forum_dm.txt asking for practical tips on achieving the 1930s look, grounded only in materials/steps from output/techniques.json.

Produce only these four files under output/. No external web use and no added facts beyond the inputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
